get.description: Check the health status of the application
Get the current status of the app configuration and onboarding process

post.description: Update the application configuration

parameters.description: New Configuration
parameters.name: newConfig

patch.description: Reload the configuration file and return the current config status

get.description: Get all available notification template variables grouped by category and by template type.
Retrieve all media items and their associated poster sets from the database, with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.description: Filter by TMDB ID (partial match)
parameters.name: item_tmdb_id

parameters.description: Filter by Library Title (partial match)
parameters.name: item_library_title

parameters.description: Filter by Year
parameters.name: item_year

parameters.description: Filter by Title (partial match)
parameters.name: item_title

parameters.description: Filter by Library Titles (comma-separated for multiple)
parameters.name: library_titles

parameters.description: Filter by Image Types (comma-separated for multiple)
parameters.name: image_types

parameters.description: Filter by Autodownload status (on, off, all)
parameters.name: autodownload

parameters.description: Filter to only items with multiple poster sets
parameters.name: multiset_only

parameters.description: Filter by Usernames (comma-separated for multiple)
parameters.name: usernames

parameters.description: Number of items per page for pagination (default: 20, -1 for no pagination)
parameters.name: items_per_page

parameters.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1)
parameters.name: page_number

parameters.description: Sort option (e.g., date_downloaded, title, year)
parameters.name: sort_option

parameters.description: Sort order (asc or desc)
parameters.name: sort_order

post.description: Add a Media Item and Poster Set to the database. If the item already exists, it will be updated with the new Media Item and Poster Set information.

parameters.description: Add Item Request
parameters.name: req

delete.description: Delete a Media Item and all associated Poster Sets from the database based on TMDB ID and Library Title.

parameters.description: TMDB ID of the Media Item
parameters.name: tmdb_id

parameters.description: Library Title of the Media Item
parameters.name: library_title

patch.description: Update a Media Item and its associated Poster Sets in the database. Poster Sets marked with "to_delete" will be removed, while others will be upserted.

parameters.description: Update Item Request
parameters.name: req

post.description: Force a check to see if any of the images need to be re-downloaded for a given Media Item and its associated Poster Sets.

parameters.description: Auto Download Force Check Request
parameters.name: req

patch.description: Mark a Media Item as ignored in the database, preventing it from being processed by other parts of the application. The ignore can be temporary or permanent based on the mode parameter.

parameters.description: TMDB ID of the Media Item
parameters.name: tmdb_id

parameters.description: Library Title of the Media Item
parameters.name: library_title

parameters.description: Ignore mode (e.g., 'always' for permanent ignore, 'until-set-available' for temporary ignore until a set is available, 'until-new-set-available' for temporary ignore until a new set is available)
parameters.name: mode

patch.description: Remove the ignored status from a Media Item in the database, allowing it to be processed by other parts of the application again.

parameters.description: TMDB ID of the Media Item
parameters.name: tmdb_id

parameters.description: Library Title of the Media Item
parameters.name: library_title

post.description: Download and apply an image for a Collection Item in the media server.

parameters.description: Download Collection Image Request
parameters.name: req

post.description: Download and apply an image for a Media Item in the media server.

parameters.description: Download Image Request
parameters.name: req

get.description: Retrieve the current status of the download queue, including the latest status message, any warnings or errors, and the timestamp of the last update. This endpoint provides insight into the overall health and activity of the download queue.
Retrieve the current items in the collection download queue, categorized by their status (in-progress, warning, error).

post.description: Add a Collection and its selected images to the collection download queue. The entry is processed by the download worker and removed from the queue once completed.

parameters.description: Queue Add Collection Item Request
parameters.name: req

delete.description: Remove a specific Collection entry from the download queue. This can be used to cancel pending collection downloads or clean up errored entries.

parameters.description: Queue Remove Collection Item Request
parameters.name: req

post.description: Retry a failed (errored) Collection entry in the download queue. The errored entry is atomically re-queued as an in-progress entry so the download worker reprocesses it on its next run.

parameters.description: Queue Retry Collection Item Request
parameters.name: req

get.description: Retrieve the current items in the download queue, categorized by their status (in-progress, warning, error). This endpoint allows clients to monitor the progress of queued download tasks and identify any issues that may have occurred during processing.

post.description: Add a Media Item and its associated Poster Sets to the download queue. The item will be processed by the download worker and removed from the queue once completed.

parameters.description: Queue Add Item Request
parameters.name: req

delete.description: Remove a specific Media Item from the download queue. This can be used to cancel pending download tasks or clean up items that are no longer needed in the queue.

parameters.description: Queue Remove Item Request
parameters.name: req

post.description: Retry a failed (errored) Media Item in the download queue. The errored entry is atomically re-queued as an in-progress entry so the download worker reprocesses it on its next run.

parameters.description: Queue Retry Item Request
parameters.name: req

get.description: Check the health status of the application
Serve a locally-imported Kometa asset by its image ID (kometa|<folder>/<file>) from the configured asset directory.

parameters.description: Kometa image ID (kometa|<folder>/<file>)
parameters.name: asset_id

get.description: Get a collection item image (poster or backdrop) from the media server by rating key and image type

parameters.description: Rating Key of the collection item
parameters.name: rating_key

parameters.description: Type of image to fetch (poster or backdrop)
parameters.name: image_type

get.description: Get a media item image (poster, backdrop or thumb) from the media server by rating key and image type

parameters.description: Rating Key of the media item
parameters.name: rating_key

parameters.description: Rating Key of the specific image to fetch (if different from the media item rating key)
parameters.name: image_rating_key

parameters.description: Type of image to fetch (poster, backdrop or thumb)
parameters.name: image_type

get.description: Get a user avatar image from Mediux by avatar ID or username

parameters.description: Avatar ID of the user to fetch the avatar for
parameters.name: avatar_id

parameters.description: Username of the user to fetch the avatar for (only used if avatar_id is not provided)
parameters.name: username

200.description: Avatar image data in JPEG format

get.description: Get an image from Mediux by asset ID, modified date and quality

parameters.description: Asset ID of the image to fetch
parameters.name: asset_id

parameters.description: Modified date of the image in ISO format (e.g. 2024-01-01T12:00:00Z)
parameters.name: modified_date

parameters.description: Quality of the image to fetch (thumb, optimized, original). Default is thumb.
parameters.name: quality

delete.description: Clear all temporary images from the server's temp-images directory. This endpoint is useful for maintenance and cleanup of temporary files that are no longer needed.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all scheduled jobs in the system, including their name, description, schedule, and next run time. This endpoint provides insight into the background tasks that are set up to run at specific intervals or times.

post.description: Trigger a specific job to run immediately by providing the job name and ID as query parameters. This endpoint allows for manual execution of scheduled jobs outside of their regular schedule, which can be useful for testing or urgent tasks.

parameters.description: Name of the Job to Run
parameters.name: job_name

parameters.description: ID of the Job to Run
parameters.name: job_id

get.description: Return whether a Kometa asset import is currently running and the result of the most recent run.

post.description: Scan the configured Kometa asset directory, upload matching assets to Plex, and record them in the database. Runs asynchronously; poll GET /api/kometa/import for progress.
Apply labels and tags to a Media Item in the media server and Sonarr/Radarr.

parameters.description: Apply Labels/Tags Request
parameters.name: req

post.description: Authenticate user and return a JWT token

parameters.description: Login Request
parameters.name: req

get.description: Retrieve log entries from the server's log file with optional filtering by log level, status, and route/action. This endpoint allows clients to access and analyze logs for monitoring and debugging purposes.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of log levels to filter by (e.g. info,error,debug)
parameters.name: log_levels

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of statuses to filter by (e.g. success,error)
parameters.name: statuses

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of route paths or action names to filter by (e.g. GET:/api/db,User Login)
parameters.name: actions

parameters.description: Number of log entries to return per page (default: 20)
parameters.name: items_per_page

parameters.description: Page number to return (default: 1)
parameters.name: page_number

delete.description: Clear log files from the server. You can choose to clear the current log file or all old log files while keeping the current one. This endpoint is useful for maintenance and managing disk space used by logs.

parameters.description: Clear Option (current or old)
parameters.name: option

get.description: Retrieve a list of movie collections from the media server. This endpoint fetches all movie collections available in the media server's movie libraries, allowing clients to display and interact with the collections of movies configured on the media server.

Retrieve all child items of a movie collection from the media server, along with their associated posters. This endpoint accepts a query parameter to identify the collection and returns the child items contained within that collection, as well as any relevant poster sets for those items. This allows clients to display the contents of a movie collection along with visual representations (posters) for each item.
get.summary: Get All Movie Collection Children Items And Posters

parameters.description: Rating Key of the Collection
parameters.name: rating_key

get.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific media item from the media server, including its metadata, associated poster sets, and user follow/hide status. This endpoint accepts a rating key as a query parameter to identify the media item and returns comprehensive details that can be used to display the media item information and related sets in the client application.

parameters.description: Rating Key of the Media Item
parameters.name: rating_key

parameters.description: Return Type (full or item, default is full)
parameters.name: return_type

get.description: Retrieve a list of library sections from the configured media server. This endpoint fetches the available library sections, including their ID, title, type, and path, allowing clients to display and interact with the media libraries configured on the media server.

post.description: Retrieve a list of library sections from a specified media server configuration. This endpoint accepts a media server configuration in the request body and returns the available library sections for that media server, allowing clients to display options for users to select which library section they want to interact with.

parameters.description: Media Server Configuration
parameters.name: request

get.description: Retrieve items from a specific library section in the media server. This endpoint accepts query parameters to identify the library section and pagination options, and returns the items contained within that library section, allowing clients to display the media items available in the selected library section.

parameters.description: Library Section ID
parameters.name: section_id

parameters.description: Library Section Title
parameters.name: section_title

parameters.description: Library Section Type
parameters.name: section_type

parameters.description: Start Index for Pagination
parameters.name: section_start_index

patch.description: Rate a media item on the media server. This endpoint allows clients to submit a user rating for a specific media item, which will be sent to the media server (currently only supported for Plex). The rating should be a number between 0 and 5, and it will be converted to the appropriate scale for the media server before being submitted.

parameters.description: The rating key of the media item to rate
parameters.name: rating_key

parameters.description: The user rating for the media item (0-5)
parameters.name: rating

post.description: Refresh the metadata of a media item on the media server. This endpoint accepts a rating_key for the media item to be refreshed and an optional refresh_rating_key to specify which metadata entry to refresh. It triggers a metadata refresh on the media server for the specified media item, allowing clients to update the displayed information for that item after changes have been made on the media server or to fix any metadata issues.

parameters.description: Rating Key of the media item to refresh
parameters.name: rating_key

parameters.description: Rating Key to specify which metadata entry to refresh
parameters.name: refresh_rating_key

get.description: Retrieve a specific item set (such as a show set for TV shows or a movie set/collection for movies) by its unique identifier. This endpoint accepts query parameters to identify the set, including the set ID, set type (show, movie, or collection), and the library section it belongs to. The response includes details about the set and any related media items that are part of the set, allowing clients to display comprehensive information about the set and its contents in the UI.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the item set
parameters.name: set_id

parameters.description: Type of the item set (show, movie, or collection)
parameters.name: set_type

parameters.description: TMDB ID of the media item (required for collection sets)
parameters.name: tmdb_id

parameters.description: Title of the library the set belongs to
parameters.name: item_library_title

get.description: Retrieve item sets for a specific media item in the library. This endpoint accepts query parameters to identify the media item and its library, and returns any related item sets (such as show sets for TV shows or movie sets/collections for movies) that the media item belongs to, allowing clients to display related items and collections in the UI.

parameters.description: TMDB ID of the media item
parameters.name: tmdb_id

parameters.description: Type of the media item (movie or show)
parameters.name: item_type

parameters.description: Title of the library the media item belongs to
parameters.name: item_library_title

get.description: Retrieve all item sets created by the specified user in Mediux. This endpoint returns an array of item sets, including details such as the set name, type (show, movie, or collection), and the items contained within each set. This allows clients to display the user's custom collections and preferences within the Mediux ecosystem in the UI.

parameters.description: Username of the user whose sets are being retrieved
parameters.name: username

get.description: Retrieve the list of users that the current user is following or hiding in Mediux. This endpoint returns an array of user information, including their username, display name, and whether they are being followed or hidden by the current user. This allows clients to display the user's social connections and preferences within the Mediux ecosystem in the UI.
get.summary: Get Mediux User Following and Hiding

get.description: Retrieve a new Plex Pin and the associated Plex ID for authentication. This endpoint is used to initiate the Plex authentication process by providing the necessary credentials for the user to authenticate their Plex account.

post.description: Check if the provided Plex ID has authenticated with Plex and retrieve available server connections if authenticated. This endpoint is used during the Plex authentication process to verify the user's Plex account and gather necessary information for integration.

parameters.description: Plex ID to check for authentication
parameters.name: plex_id

post.description: Apply saved collection sets to a movie newly imported by Radarr.

parameters.description: Library title the movie belongs to
parameters.name: library

get.description: Perform a search across media items, collection items, Mediux users, and saved sets based on the provided query and filters. The search supports filtering by year, library section, and unique identifiers (TMDB ID or RatingKey) using specific query syntax. The response includes matching media items, collection items, Mediux users, and saved sets, along with metadata about the last full update for each category to help clients determine if they need to refresh their cached data.

parameters.description: The search query string with optional filters (e.g., 'Inception y:2010 l:Movies id:12345')
parameters.name: query

parameters.description: Whether to include media items in the search results
parameters.name: search_media_items

parameters.description: Whether to include collection items in the search results
parameters.name: search_collection_items

parameters.description: Whether to include Mediux users in the search results
parameters.name: search_mediux_users

parameters.description: Whether to include saved sets in the search results
parameters.name: search_saved_sets

post.description: Validate the provided media server information by attempting to connect to the media server. This endpoint is used during the onboarding process to ensure that the media server settings entered by the user are correct and that a connection can be established. The response will indicate whether the connection was successful and provide details about the media server if it was validated successfully.

parameters.description: Media Server Information to Validate
parameters.name: media_server

post.description: Validate the provided Mediux API token by attempting to connect to the Mediux site. This endpoint is used during the onboarding process to ensure that the Mediux settings entered by the user are correct and that a connection can be established. The response will indicate whether the connection was successful.

parameters.description: Mediux Information to Validate
parameters.name: mediux_info

post.description: Send a test notification using the specified notification provider. This endpoint is used to verify that the notification settings are correct and that notifications can be sent successfully. The request should include the notification provider information, and the response will indicate whether the test notification was sent successfully or if there were any errors.

parameters.description: Notification Provider Information for sending the test notification
parameters.name: provider

post.description: Validate the provided Sonarr/Radarr information by attempting to connect to the application. This endpoint is used during the onboarding process to ensure that the Sonarr/Radarr settings entered by the user are correct and that a connection can be established. The response will indicate whether the connection was successful and provide details about the application if it was validated successfully.

parameters.description: Sonarr/Radarr Information to Validate
parameters.name: sonarr_radarr_info

post.description: Validate the provided Sonarr/Radarr information by attempting to connect to the application. This endpoint is used during the onboarding process to ensure that the Sonarr/Radarr settings entered by the user are correct and that a connection can be established. The response will indicate whether the connection was successful and provide details about the application if it was validated successfully.

parameters.description: Sonarr/Radarr Information to Validate
parameters.name: sonarr_radarr_info

sonarr_radarr.description: List of Sonarr/Radarr instances to integrate with.

enabled.description: Whether to enable caching of images.

include_image.description: Whether to include an image with the custom notification.

dsn.description: Data Source Name for the database connection (if applicable).

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the database server (if applicable).

name.description: Name of the database to connect to.

path.description: File path for the database (if applicable, e.g., for SQLite).

port.description: Port number of the database server (if applicable).

kometa.description: Settings for Kometa asset directory integration (Plex only).

save_images_locally.description: Settings for saving images locally alongside content.

asset_directory.description: Path to the Kometa asset directory (the same directory Kometa reads assets from). Uses the folder-per-item (asset_folders: true) layout.

enabled.description: Whether to write downloaded images into the Kometa asset directory using Kometa naming conventions. Plex exclusive feature.

import_cron.description: Optional cron expression for periodically importing existing Kometa assets. Empty means import is manual only.

library_asset_folders.description: Optional per-library subfolder (relative to AssetDirectory) keyed by Plex library title. When set, a library's assets are written to <AssetDirectory>/<subfolder>/<item>/... instead of directly under AssetDirectory. Must match the per-library asset_directory configured in Kometa itself. A library with no entry keeps writing flat to AssetDirectory.

sonarr_radarr_fallback.description: When a media-server lookup fails (e.g. Plex returns a 404) but the item exists in Sonarr/Radarr, still write the downloaded images into the Kometa asset directory, deriving the asset folder name from the Sonarr/Radarr path. Requires Enabled. Plex exclusive feature.

remove_overlay_label_only_on_poster_down.description: Whether to remove the "Overlay" label from media items after downloading a poster image. This is to allow Kometa to reprocess the image and apply the overlays. This is a Plex exclusive feature and should be set to true if you have "Overlay" in your "Remove" list for the Plex application under "LabelsAndTags".

level.description: Logging level (e.g., TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR).

api_token.description: Authentication token for accessing the media server.

enable_plex_event_listener.description: Whether to enable the Plex event listener for reapplying images on refresh. Plex exclusive feature.

enable_sort_by_episode_added_date.description: Whether to check episodes for added date when getting Media Items. This is only for Plex servers.

libraries.description: List of media server libraries to manage.

type.description: Type of media server (e.g., plex, emby, jellyfin).

url.description: Base URL of the media server. This is either the IP:Port or the domain name (e.g., plex.domain.com).

user_id.description: User ID for accessing the media server. This is used for Emby and Jellyfin servers.

api_token.description: Authentication token for accessing MediUX services.

download_quality.description: Quality of the media to download from MediUX (Options: "original", "optimized") Defaults to "optimized".

app_startup.description: Custom notification settings for application startup.

autodownload.description: Custom notification settings for auto-download events.

check_for_media_item_changes_job.description: Custom notification settings for the media item changes job.

download_queue.description: Custom notification settings for download queue events.

new_sets_available_for_ignored_items.description: Custom notification settings for when new sets become available for ignored items.

sonarr_notification.description: Custom notification settings for Sonarr events.

test_notification.description: Custom notification settings for test notifications.

webhook.description: Webhook URL for the Discord notification provider.

api_token.description: Token for the Gotify notification provider.

url.description: URL for the Gotify notification provider.

enabled.description: Whether this notification method is enabled

api_token.description: Token for the Pushover notification provider.

user_key.description: UserKey for the Pushover notification provider.

headers.description: Headers for the Webhook notification provider.

url.description: URL for the Webhook notification provider.

enabled.description: Whether this notification method is enabled

templates.description: Custom notification templates for different events

enabled.description: Whether to save images next to their content.

episode_naming_convention.description: Episode naming convention for the media server. Only needed for Plex. Will default to match

path.description: By default, this is set to alongside the content. If set, this will override that behavior and save all images to this path.

running_on_windows.description: Whether the application is running on Windows. This affects path formatting.

api_token.description: API key for accessing the Sonarr/Radarr server.

library.description: Name of the Media Server library associated with this Sonarr/Radarr instance.

url.description: Base URL of the Sonarr/Radarr server.

applications.description: List of Sonarr/Radarr applications to integrate with.

images_skipped_owned.description: assets not uploaded because a MediUX set owns their image type

managed_by_aura.description: one or more image types were skipped to protect MediUX selections

images_skipped_owned.description: assets not uploaded because a MediUX set owns their image type

skipped_managed_by_aura.description: items where at least one image type was protected

elapsed_us.description: Elapsed time for the action in microseconds

name.description: Human name for the action: "Query DB", "Call External API"

timestamp.description: When the action started (useful for ordering)

actions.description: Slice of actions within this operation

message.description: Human name for the overall operation: "Add to DB", "Fetch All Media Items" or Route.Path

route.description: Optional route metadata (nil for background jobs)

time.description: Time of the log entry (same as Timestamp, kept for compatibility)

help.example: Check server logs for more details

params.description: Query/path/route params: map[string][]string to preserve multiple values

response_bytes.description: Number of bytes written in the response (middleware can capture)

date_updated.description: Last update for the Movie/Show info

status.description: Status of the item (returning, ended, etc.)

popularity_global.description: Global popularity score of the set

set_ids.description: IDs of sets (show, movie, collection) in this boxset

index.description: Unique identifier for the collection in Plex

failed_at.description: FailedAt is when the entry was moved to its terminal error/warning state.

queue_errors.description: QueueErrors lists the fatal reasons the entry failed to apply.

queue_warnings.description: QueueWarnings lists non-fatal issues recorded while processing the entry.

included_items.description: Map of included items by TMDB ID

popularity_global.description: Global popularity score of the set

to_delete.description: Flag to indicate if the poster set should be deleted (Not used in DB)

failed_at.description: FailedAt is when the entry was moved to the error/warning state.

queue_errors.description: QueueErrors lists the fatal reasons the entry failed to download.

queue_warnings.description: QueueWarnings lists non-fatal issues recorded while processing the entry.

file_size.description: Size of the file in bytes

id.description: Unique asset ID for the poster file

item_tmdb_id.description: TMDB ID of the parent item

season_number.description: Present for Season Posters and Titlecards

src.description: Source URL (ID+Date - calculated by MediUX API)

media_item.description: Full media item info from Aura

id.description: Unique identifier for the library section.

paths.description: Paths of the library section on the media server.

content_rating.description: Used in MediaItem Details Page - For more information about the item

db_saved_sets.description: Used in MediaItem Details Page - For poster sets

guids.description: Used in MediaItem Details Page - For ratings

has_mediux_sets.description: Used in Home Page - sorting and filtering

ignored_in_db.description: Whether the item is marked as ignored

ignored_sets.description: List of set IDs that were present when the item was ignored (used for "until-new-set-available" mode)

library_title.description: Title of the library/section the item belongs to

movie.description: Present if Type is "movie"; Contains file info

rating_key.description: RatingKey is the internal ID from the media server

series.description: Present if Type is "show"; Contains seasons and episodes info

summary.description: Summary or description of the media item

tmdb_id.description: TMDB ID of the media item

year.description: Release year of the media item

included_items.description: Map of included items by TMDB ID

item_ids.description: IDs of items in the set

popularity_global.description: Global popularity score of the set

app_fully_loaded.description: Whether the app is fully loaded and ready to use

app_loading_step.description: Current loading step of the app

config_loaded.description: Whether a config file is loaded

config_valid.description: Whether the loaded config is valid

media_server_name.description: Friendly name of the media server

needs_setup.description: Whether the app needs initial setup

add_to_db_only.description: If true, the item will be added to the database but not have any labels or tags applied. This is for users who want to manage labels and tags manually.

complete.description: Whether the provided data is complete or if we need to fetch missing information

current_sets.description: comma-separated list of current sets for the item, used for temporary ignore modes
